title: Phone Registration Status (Cisco CUCM)
dataSources: UCM device status, RISPORT real-time data
spl:
index=voip sourcetype="cisco:ucm:syslog"
| search "DeviceUnregistered" OR "StationDeregister"
| timechart span=5m count as deregistrations
| where deregistrations > 10
description: Mass phone de-registration indicates network or UCM issues affecting the entire communications infrastructure.
value: Mass phone de-registration indicates network or UCM issues affecting the entire communications infrastructure.
implementation: Poll UCM RISPORT API for device registration status or forward UCM syslog. Alert on mass de-registrations (>10 devices in 5 minutes). Track registration counts per site. Monitor SRST fallback activations.
visualization: Single value (registered phones), Line chart (registration count trend), Table (recently de-registered devices).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: UCM syslog, RISPORT API.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: UCM device status, RISPORT real-time data.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll UCM RISPORT API for device registration status or forward UCM syslog. Alert on mass de-registrations (>10 devices in 5 minutes). Track registration counts per site. Monitor SRST fallback activations.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=voip sourcetype="cisco:ucm:syslog"
| search "DeviceUnregistered" OR "StationDeregister"
| timechart span=5m count as deregistrations
| where deregistrations > 10
```

Understanding this SPL

**Phone Registration Status (Cisco CUCM)** — Mass phone de-registration indicates network or UCM issues affecting the entire communications infrastructure.

Documented **Data sources**: UCM device status, RISPORT real-time data. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): UCM syslog, RISPORT API. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: voip; **sourcetype**: cisco:ucm:syslog. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=voip, sourcetype="cisco:ucm:syslog". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=5m** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Filters the current rows with `where deregistrations > 10` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with CUCM RTMT, CallManager service parameters, and CDR/CMR or quality reports in Cisco unified communications admin for the same cluster and interval.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (registered phones), Line chart (registration count trend), Table (recently de-registered devices).
grandmaExplanation: We use this to stay ahead of email and collaboration problems, so the team is not the last to know when something drifts or breaks.
hardware: Cisco Unified Communications Manager (CUCM), Unity Connection, IP Phone 7800 series, IP Phone 8800 series
knownFalsePositives: Phone reboots, firmware updates, and DHCP renewals that drop registration for seconds.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
